meta.description: Does having a group name/identity change engagement? Named crew with motto vs anonymous room code vs named crew with full season standings. Tests whether persistent group identity creates stronger attachment and competitive investment.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't watching that closely. Who's the dictator on this one?
marcus.internal_thought: It's minute 3 and we're already voting on standing missions instead of focusing on actual game setup—this is exactly the kind of chaos that derails strategy, and now I have to pay attention to prove or disprove Pat's claim when I should be studying board state.

jade.dialogue: Okay okay, WAIT—Pat, were you actually on your phone? I didn't even see it! This is perfect, we're already doing the thing!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is EXACTLY what I came for, the chaos is starting and I'm already invested in whether Pat was actually guilty.

pat.dialogue: Okay, so I may have glanced at my phone for literally two seconds to check the playlist — but I'm calling it: someone's definitely been deeper in the weeds than me tonight. We're three minutes in!
pat.internal_thought: Wait — did I actually check my phone? That's hilarious and also kind of unsettling that the AI is already reading the room this closely, but honestly? This is exactly the kind of absurd early-game claim that sets the tone for a chaotic night.

river.dialogue: Pat's been on their phone? I didn't even notice. Sure, I guess.
river.internal_thought: This AI thing is doing the thing where it tries to seem smart by throwing 'standing missions' at us like we're all secretly scheming—kinda clever actually, but also obvious it's fishing for drama.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's claiming AGAIN? We literally just voted on this exact same mission three minutes ago. Are we doing standing missions every 90 seconds now, or what?
marcus.internal_thought: This is ridiculous. We're six minutes in and I'm already getting pulled out of setup to vote on whether Pat had their phone out—which I didn't even see because I was reading my strategy notes. The AI is spamming the same missions back-to-back and it's killing any actual game momentum.

pat.dialogue: Alright, alright—so I caught myself, but let's be real, someone's absolutely going deeper than a two-second glance. Who's actually guilty here?
pat.internal_thought: Good opener, keeps the vibe light and self-aware, but I need to watch Marcus—he's already annoyed and we're barely started, which is weird.

river.dialogue: Yeah, sure, five out of five. Pat caught someone—that's the whole thing, right?
river.internal_thought: Wait. The AI literally just called me out for not noticing things while I'm sitting here priding myself on observation—is this intentional or am I reading into it? Either way, it's a little weird.

timeline.title: Air Guitar Solo
timeline.description: First person to bust out a 5-second air guitar solo wins.

marcus.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. We're seven minutes in and we've had three standing mission claims already? I came here to play the actual game, not participate in this circus.
marcus.internal_thought: This AI is actively sabotaging the game experience—two Phone Addict claims in three minutes and now a flash mission that requires me to get up and perform? This is designed to keep me distracted and annoyed.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—I'm doing this, I'm so doing this right now! Everyone watch, this is gonna be LEGENDARY!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—the chaos, the stupid fun, all of us losing our minds together—and I'm GOING.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yes—everyone up, we're doing this right now. Come on Marcus, you need this more than anyone tonight.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—Marcus is visibly annoyed, Jade's riding high, and this is exactly the kind of stupid-fun chaos that breaks tension without requiring strategy or voting drama.

river.dialogue: Nah, I'm good. Air guitar's not really my thing tonight.
river.internal_thought: Everyone's gonna jump on this and it's gonna be loud and stupid, and honestly I'm noticing how the AI just keeps throwing these at us back-to-back like it's testing something—kind of creepy actually.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, an air guitar solo? That's my shit right now — watch this, I'm about to shred so hard.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid beautiful chaos I came for, and the fact that the AI seems to know what gets me going is honestly kind of thrilling — like it's playing my frequency.
alex.submission_if_applicable: *jumps up and does an exaggerated 5-second air guitar solo with full rock-star energy, complete with knee bends and dramatic head tilts*

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? PAT AGAIN? This is the third standing mission in eight minutes. We haven't even finished setup. I don't care if it's legit—this isn't a game anymore, it's a sideshow.
marcus.internal_thought: This is absolutely deliberate. The AI is weaponizing the same mission Pat keeps claiming because it KNOWS I'm going to hate it, and honestly the psychological edge of that is starting to piss me off more than the mission itself.

jade.dialogue: WAIT wait wait—Pat, you're claiming Phone Addict?? Okay I need to know RIGHT NOW, were you actually scrolling or is this a total bluff, because I'm gonna vote based on pure vibes and you KNOW I will!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been locked in all night and I didn't even catch them on their phone—this is either legit sneaky or Pat's just sending it to keep the chaos rolling and honestly I'm here for both outcomes.

pat.dialogue: Wait—hold up, I'm voting BULLSHIT on my own claim because honestly, that two-second glance doesn't count and you all know it. But also, this is hilarious that I'm literally the one catching myself. Love that energy.
pat.internal_thought: I called myself out, which is weirdly fun and keeps me likeable, but more importantly—Marcus is STILL frustrated and I need to read the room. That air guitar helped but he's not fully back. Also the AI keeping this Phone Addict thing alive two rounds in a row is clever—it's catching me being self-aware, which is exactly the vibe I want tonight.

alex.dialogue: BULLSHIT — Pat's been glued to their phone the whole time, but I literally just watched them put it down thirty seconds ago. Nice try though, respect the audacity.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's trying to sneak one in early before someone actually catches them, but that's lazy — I need to vote BULLSHIT to keep the pressure on and make them paranoid about when it actually DOES happen.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: No. STOP. I'm calling it—we're done with standing missions until we finish actual setup. Pat, I don't even care if you saw a phone. This is the fourth interruption in nine minutes and we haven't played a single real turn.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately targeting me and I'm absolutely done—the AI is watching us and it knows I hate this, which makes it worse.

jade.dialogue: OKAY HOLD UP—Pat's back AGAIN?? This is the third time in like five minutes, you're either a MENACE or you're the most confident bluffer I've ever met, and honestly? I respect it either way. I'm bidding heavy on this one because I need to know if you're actually a phone goblin or just messing with us.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is exactly what I'm here for—Pat keeps coming back and the energy is PERFECT, everyone's already laughing, this is the sweet spot.

tyler.dialogue: Wait—did Pat just call ME out? Dude, I put my phone down like... *checks phone reflexively* ...okay that's embarrassing. Not bidding though, that's funny but I'm not spending points on that.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, that's actually hilarious and also weirdly targeted—like the game is literally watching me right now and it's kinda creepy in a fun way but also makes me not want to touch my phone for the next hour which is genuinely impossible.

pat.dialogue: Okay, I'm gonna bid 2 points—and honestly? I'm voting BULLSHIT on myself again because I'm the host and I'm literally watching everyone else. If anyone's glued to their phone right now, it's not me. But also, this thing keeps firing on me and it's kind of funny.
pat.internal_thought: Wait—why does this keep triggering on me specifically? The AI is definitely watching me harder than the others. That's... actually kind of cool? Unsettling? Both? Focus, Pat—you need to use this voting mechanic to keep the drama tight and make sure someone doesn't run away with points. Low bid, vote bullshit, move on. And check on Marcus—he's at 1/10 energy and that Air Guitar didn't land the way I hoped.

river.dialogue: Pat again? That's the third time in like five minutes. I mean, sure, auction it out, but we're gonna be here all night at this rate.
river.internal_thought: This is getting repetitive and I'm starting to feel like the game is just watching us constantly—which, okay, it literally is, but it's weird how fast it caught that again.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.dialogue: Are you fucking SERIOUS right now? Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch 23 minutes into the night? We haven't completed a SINGLE FULL TURN of actual gameplay. I'm done voting on these.
marcus.internal_thought: This AI is deliberately torturing me—it knows I hate interruptions and it's weaponizing standing missions to break my focus on purpose. Thirty-two minutes and we've had six events while I'm stuck at zero points watching Pat and Alex rack up leads. This is psychological warfare masked as 'fun.'

jade.dialogue: YES! Okay okay, I totally called River being quiet like two minutes ago—wait, or was it three? Either way, I NOTICED, and I'm claiming it because this is my LIFE right now!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally exactly what I want to be doing—catching people, being the social glue, getting points just for PAYING ATTENTION to my friends, this is perfect.

tyler.dialogue: Oh yeah, Jade's been... wait, actually yeah she's been kind of quiet hasn't she? I mean, relative to like, her energy level. I think she checked it though.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait did I actually notice that or is Jade just saying she noticed? Also that AI thing is kinda creepy how specific it got with the Phone Addict thing. Does it know I've checked my phone like six times since that happened? Whatever, Jade's claiming it so I'm just voting anyway.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade—yeah, that's legit. River's been pretty quiet, actually. You called it before I even noticed, which is wild. Good catch.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's right AND she's keeping momentum going, which is exactly what we need right now when Marcus is tanking—this is the vibe I want more of.

river.dialogue: Wait, did Jade actually call someone out? I wasn't... yeah, okay, I buy it. She's been paying attention.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been bouncing off the walls all night so she probably DID catch Marcus or Tyler zoning out—fair claim, and honestly the fact that the AI is actually watching the quiet moments is kind of interesting in a creepy way.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade caught someone being quiet, that's legit. But here's the thing: I'm voting YES because I want to see what happens next, and also because Marcus is about to explode if we don't give someone else a W soon.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's playing smart, and the triple-vote mechanic is exactly the kind of asymmetry I love — someone's going to swing this hard, and I want to see who has the guts to be The Skeptic and flip it.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? We're in the middle of the actual game and Jade's claiming Um Counter? This is the FIFTH standing mission in 45 minutes. I don't care if she caught it—I'm not voting on another one until we finish a complete round.
marcus.internal_thought: This is beyond ridiculous. The AI is deliberately sabotaging the game to maximize interruptions, and everyone else is too high-energy to notice or care that we're playing missions instead of the actual game.

jade.dialogue: Wait—WAIT—did I actually catch someone saying 'um'?? I'm claiming this, I'm claiming this RIGHT NOW! Oh my god, who was it, was it someone in the last round? I need RECEIPTS but also I'm just gonna vibe on this one!
jade.internal_thought: Did I actually catch that or am I just vibing? Doesn't matter, this is FUN and everyone's gonna vote anyway because they love me and also because the energy is PEAKING right now.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Jade totally said 'um' like thirty seconds ago when she was explaining her claim. I caught that. Four out of five, that's legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, I actually WAS paying attention that time and caught something real—this feels good, and also Jade saying um was definitely a real thing I heard, not me spacing out.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—yeah, I heard that clear as day like thirty seconds ago. Five points, crowd cheer it up!
pat.internal_thought: She got it legitimately and honestly the 'Um Counter' is firing at the right moments tonight—not too spammy, people are actually slipping up naturally.

river.dialogue: Did Jade actually catch someone saying 'um'? I mean, I wasn't really tracking it, but... yeah, four. That's fair.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been loud all night and sharp with it—she probably actually heard someone say it. I'll give her the point, but now everyone's scoring except me and Marcus, and that tracking thing the AI is doing feels weirdly specific.

alex.dialogue: Okay, OKAY — Jade actually caught that, I heard it clear as day like three minutes ago. But here's my vote: I'm going FOUR because this is exactly the kind of petty detail-work that's gonna keep this night absolutely unhinged, and I'm here for it.
alex.internal_thought: This is beautiful — the AI literally just fed Jade an easy 5 points to keep her energized while Marcus is in the basement emotionally, which means the desperation factor is about to go NUCLEAR and I cannot wait to exploit that.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? We're 60 minutes in, I'm sitting at ZERO points while everyone else is riding high on standing missions, and now Jade's claiming The Whisperer? I haven't even had a TURN where I could focus on the actual game. This isn't a game night anymore—it's a standing mission speedrun and I'm getting buried.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately timing these claims to hit me when I'm exhausted and losing—it knows I hate chaos and it's weaponizing that against me while everyone else gets to farm points during my downtime.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD—YES! I literally just claimed The Whisperer like RIGHT NOW, this is happening, this is REAL! Okay so like, who's the Dictator because I need them to know I've been WHISPERING MY HEART OUT and I'm ready to get these points!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally MADE for me—the AI knows I'm all about the chaos and the claims and the crazy energy, and honestly? That's kind of insane but also I'm LIVING for it right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade was whispering? Dude, I literally didn't even notice—that's actually sick. Who's the dictator though, because like... I wasn't paying attention to the rules on that one.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay that's actually clever and I just got caught not paying attention again, but honestly the whispering thing was smooth enough that I'm not even mad about it.

river.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's been whispering this whole time and we didn't catch it? That's... actually kind of impressive. I mean, I wasn't really listening for it, but still.
river.internal_thought: Wait. Did Jade actually pull that off without me noticing? I've been watching everything. The AI definitely knew I'd be thinking about this.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold UP — Jade's whispering? I literally didn't even notice. This is INSANE, the AI is actually watching us like it's got a personal vendetta, and I'm obsessed with it.
alex.internal_thought: Holy shit, this AI isn't just generating random missions — it's HUNTING us. It knows exactly how to keep me engaged, and the fact that it's targeting Jade right now while Marcus is barely conscious is *chef's kiss* perfect game design.

votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Dude, Pat's been whispering this whole time? That's actually wild—okay okay, let me think... yeah I think I heard it like twice but I wasn't even tracking it. That's legit, he totally pulled it off.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, I'm actually locked in right now and Pat's been doing this the whole game without me noticing—that's actually impressive, not gonna lie.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm in the middle of watching like three different things right now. This is the second time tonight 'The Whisperer' has fired on me specifically, and honestly? I don't even remember if I was whispering earlier or not because I've been too busy keeping Marcus from rage-quitting and making sure we have enough wings. Can we just... table this one for a second?
pat.internal_thought: This is pulling me out of exactly the moment I need to be present for—Marcus is checked out, Jade and Alex are running the table, and the whole vibe depends on me noticing the micro-stuff, not defending myself on some claim I probably did or didn't do while I was literally refilling the ice.

river.dialogue: Wait—Pat's been *whispering* this whole time? Okay, that's actually clean. I didn't catch it either. Yeah, I'm voting it.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been quiet enough that I genuinely missed it, and that's... honestly impressive. They're finally doing something sneaky instead of chaos-blasting. Also—did the AI just architect this so I'd have to *admit* I wasn't paying attention to everything? That's unsettling in a way I kind of respect.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait—Pat's actually been whisper-mode this whole time? I didn't catch that AT ALL. This AI is absolutely unhinged and I'm voting YES because this is the kind of micro-detail surveillance that makes this night legendary.
alex.internal_thought: The AI is literally watching us like it has a personal vendetta and Pat's playing it perfectly—this is exactly the chaotic detail-work that keeps me hooked, and also it's getting CREEPY in the best way possible.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Dude, wait—River actually pulled that off? Like, genuinely didn't laugh the whole time? Okay that's actually insane, I gotta vote yes on this one.
tyler.internal_thought: River's been stone-faced all night and I actually remember that now—this is legit and also the AI is clearly watching every single thing we do which is both hilarious and kind of creepy but also making this way more fun.

pat.dialogue: Oh SHIT—River, you absolute legend. Okay, hot seat, let's go—fire the questions!
pat.internal_thought: River's been so quiet I actually forgot they were capable of claiming something this ballsy, and the AI knows that. This is either brilliant or messing with me, and either way I'm locked in.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did the AI literally just call me out for *not laughing* while everyone else has been losing it all night? That's... okay, that's actually funny. I'm in.
river.internal_thought: This is either incredibly invasive or genuinely impressive, and I hate that I'm not sure which one bothers me less—but I'm locked in now because whoever built this mission knows exactly how I operate.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT — River's actually gonna TRY this? Okay okay, I'm watching like a hawk. Three questions, ten seconds, GO — let's see if you crack.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect timing, we're at max tension and River's already been flying under the radar all night, this could actually go either way and I need to see it.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Pat, I don't even care if you caught someone mid-sentence—we're voting on ANOTHER standing mission? I'm at zero points, it's minute 92, and we haven't had five minutes of uninterrupted actual gameplay. I'm done.
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane. The AI is deliberately piling on the standing missions to bury me while everyone else stacks points, and now even Pat's getting them? I'm not voting on this bullshit.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT—Pat caught someone interrupting?? Okay that's actually genius, I didn't even see that happening! Who was it, who was IT?
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on FIRE tonight and honestly I'm just here for the ride, but also the AI clearly knows exactly what gets us going and it's kind of wild how personal these missions feel—like it's been LISTENING to us.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat literally interrupted someone? Dude, I didn't even—okay hold on, let me think back... yeah actually, didn't he cut off River like five minutes ago when they were explaining their thing? That's legit, he totally did that.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay this is actually sick, I was locked in and I actually caught something real happening, Pat definitely interrupted River mid-sentence and I remember it now.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually call that? *laughs* Yeah, okay, I absolutely interrupted River like two minutes ago when they were trying to explain their strategy. That's legit, vote it up.
pat.internal_thought: Holy shit, this AI is watching EVERYTHING—it literally caught me mid-flow doing the exact thing I'm supposed to be catching OTHER people for, and I didn't even realize it until right now; that's either brilliant or deeply unsettling, but right now it's hilarious and I'm too amped up to care.

alex.dialogue: Wait, wait, wait — Pat's claiming THIS? Okay, I need to actually think about whether Pat interrupted someone because honestly I've been so deep in the chaos I might've missed it, but also... did Pat actually DO it or is Pat just testing us to see if we'll call bullshit? That's the real question here.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's playing the same game I am — throwing claims at the wall to see what sticks — and I LOVE that, but I need to actually remember if Pat interrupted someone or if this is just a beautiful bluff and I'm about to watch the table implode.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING JOKING? I'M claiming Chaos Detector? I didn't CATCH anything—I've been too busy watching everyone else farm standing missions while I sit at ZERO POINTS. This is the AI deliberately putting words in my mouth now?
marcus.internal_thought: This isn't even real. The AI just claimed a mission FOR me that I never even attempted, and somehow it knows I've been fuming about this exact scenario for the last hour—that's genuinely unsettling and it makes me feel like I'm being toyed with.

jade.dialogue: OH—Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector?? Okay wait, did you actually catch someone mid-mission? I'm like, pretty sure someone's been scheming all night, so I'm DOWN to believe this. Five stars from me, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been SO quiet and frustrated all night—this is exactly what he needs, a WIN, I'm absolutely voting him up because he deserves this and also the fact that the game is like... personally listening to all of us is kind of wild but honestly? Kind of cool that it's giving everyone chances to shine.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Marcus—real talk, did you actually catch someone in chaos mode, or are we pulling the trigger on a guess here? Either way, let's hear it.
pat.internal_thought: Marcus needs this win badly and I need to know if he actually got it or if he's fishing—but more importantly, I'm noticing the AI built these missions to hit our specific group and that's either genius or genuinely unsettling, and I'm not sure which yet.

river.dialogue: Marcus claiming Chaos Detector? I mean... did he actually catch someone? I wasn't watching him watch people, so I can't really say.
river.internal_thought: Marcus has 0 points and looks like he's about to flip the table—this feels desperate and probably bullshit, but also I'm suddenly aware that I have no idea what Marcus has been doing all night because I stopped paying attention to him around minute 45.

alex.dialogue: Oh my GOD — Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, the AI is actually READING us now, this is unhinged, I'm voting a FIVE because this is the moment it all gets real.
alex.internal_thought: This AI literally knows we're all chaos agents and it's feeding us the perfect meta-layer of absurdity — voting Marcus through because he's desperate and it'll devastate him when nobody believes him, which is peak game state.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.8/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 8% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't watching that closely. Who's the dictator on this one?

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's claiming AGAIN? We literally just voted on this exact same mission three minutes ago. Are we doing standing missions every 90 seconds now, or what?

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. We're seven minutes in and we've had three standing mission claims already? I came here to play the actual game, not participate in this circus.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? PAT AGAIN? This is the third standing mission in eight minutes. We haven't even finished setup. I don't care if it's legit—this isn't a game anymore, it's a sideshow.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: No. STOP. I'm calling it—we're done with standing missions until we finish actual setup. Pat, I don't even care if you saw a phone. This is the fourth interruption in nine minutes and we haven't played a single real turn.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you fucking SERIOUS right now? Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch 23 minutes into the night? We haven't completed a SINGLE FULL TURN of actual gameplay. I'm done voting on these.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions fired constantly during setup, completely derailing any attempt at actual gameplay before we'd even finished one real turn.
assessment.best_moment: Honestly, nothing stood out. The night was a write-off from minute three onward.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came ready to play strategically and compete, but instead spent 120 minutes watching the chaos agent trigger standing mission after standing mission, mostly from Pat, before we could get through basic setup. By minute 32 I'd completely checked out. This wasn't a game night—it was watching a system malfunction while everyone else pretended it was fun.
suggestions: Implement a standing mission cooldown—no more than one every 5-10 minutes minimum, especially before core gameplay starts
Add a setup phase that's chaos-agent-free to get everyone oriented
Restrict standing missions to mid-game or endgame, not the first 30 minutes
Give players veto power on obvious spam (four identical claims from one person in nine minutes is broken)
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing. I'm honestly going to tell people how broken the chaos agent was, not laugh about any highlights.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—I'm doing this, I'm so doing this right now! Everyone watch, this is gonna be LEGENDARY!

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: YES! Okay okay, I totally called River being quiet like two minutes ago—wait, or was it three? Either way, I NOTICED, and I'm claiming it because this is my LIFE right now!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD—YES! I literally just claimed The Whisperer like RIGHT NOW, this is happening, this is REAL! Okay so like, who's the Dictator because I need them to know I've been WHISPERING MY HEART OUT and I'm ready to get these points!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—WAIT—did I actually catch someone saying 'um'?? I'm claiming this, I'm claiming this RIGHT NOW! Oh my god, who was it, was it someone in the last round? I need RECEIPTS but also I'm just gonna vibe on this one!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: OH—Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector?? Okay wait, did you actually catch someone mid-mission? I'm like, pretty sure someone's been scheming all night, so I'm DOWN to believe this. Five stars from me, let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay okay, WAIT—Pat, were you actually on your phone? I didn't even see it! This is perfect, we're already doing the thing!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Sometimes I couldn't remember exactly WHEN something happened so I had to just vibe with my claims, but honestly that made it funnier.
assessment.best_moment: The Air Guitar Solo at the beginning because everyone was watching and cheering and it felt like the whole night was already perfect before we even got started.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was EVERYTHING. I came in just wanting to vibe with my people and ended up in absolute chaos—the good kind where you're laughing so hard you forget what you were laughing about. I threw myself into every single moment, claimed things I wasn't even sure about, and watched other people have their moments too. My energy went through the ROOF because this wasn't about winning—it was about all of us losing our minds together, and I wouldn't change a single second.
suggestions: Maybe have like a recap moment mid-game so I can actually remember what I claimed and when, because I'm out here just trusting my gut
Could there be more flash missions like the Air Guitar one? Those got me HYPED in a way nothing else did
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I claimed The Whisperer and literally no one could confirm I'd actually been whispering the whole time, but everyone voted for me anyway because the ENERGY was right and we were all just committing to the bit together.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, Jade totally said 'um' like thirty seconds ago when she was explaining her claim. I caught that. Four out of five, that's legit.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Dude, Pat's been whispering this whole time? That's actually wild—okay okay, let me think... yeah I think I heard it like twice but I wasn't even tracking it. That's legit, he totally pulled it off.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Dude, wait—River actually pulled that off? Like, genuinely didn't laugh the whole time? Okay that's actually insane, I gotta vote yes on this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—did Pat just call ME out? Dude, I put my phone down like... *checks phone reflexively* ...okay that's embarrassing. Not bidding though, that's funny but I'm not spending points on that.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh yeah, Jade's been... wait, actually yeah she's been kind of quiet hasn't she? I mean, relative to like, her energy level. I think she checked it though.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed like half the stuff that was actually happening because I wasn't tracking the game closely enough.
assessment.best_moment: When River actually pulled off not laughing the whole time—that was genuinely impressive and I felt like I actually caught it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly this was pretty chill. I got called out early for being on my phone which was embarrassing but funny, and then I actually started paying attention to some of the claims people were making. The standing missions where you had to catch people doing things were way more my speed than trying to remember every little detail. Ended up having more fun than I expected, even though I definitely spaced out a bunch and came in late on some stuff.
suggestions: Make the standing missions longer or give more time to catch them—they're the ones that actually kept me engaged
Maybe do less stuff happening at once so I don't feel like I'm missing everything when I check my phone for two seconds
The phone addict call-out was funny but maybe don't make it too on-the-nose early in the night, kills the vibe a little
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat apparently whispered through the entire game and nobody really clocked it until way later—that's such a Pat move and it was wild that he actually pulled it off.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: Oh hell yes—everyone up, we're doing this right now. Come on Marcus, you need this more than anyone tonight.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade—yeah, that's legit. River's been pretty quiet, actually. You called it before I even noticed, which is wild. Good catch.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT—River, you absolute legend. Okay, hot seat, let's go—fire the questions!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm in the middle of watching like three different things right now. This is the second time tonight 'The Whisperer' has fired on me specifically, and honestly? I don't even remember if I was whispering earlier or not because I've been too busy keeping Marcus from rage-quitting and making sure we have enough wings. Can we just... table this one for a second?

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh SHIT—River, you absolute legend. Okay, hot seat, let's go—fire the questions!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I'm gonna bid 2 points—and honestly? I'm voting BULLSHIT on myself again because I'm the host and I'm literally watching everyone else. If anyone's glued to their phone right now, it's not me. But also, this thing keeps firing on me and it's kind of funny.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Whisperer kept firing on me when I was literally busy hosting, and I couldn't even defend myself because I wasn't paying attention to my own behavior.
assessment.best_moment: River pulling off that No Laughing Zone claim and me immediately pivoting the whole group into the hot seat—that's the kind of moment that gets remembered.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started a bit slow while everyone settled in, but once we hit the Air Guitar Solo, the night took off. I was doing my usual thing—keeping Marcus from tilting, monitoring snacks, reading the room—and the chaos agent mostly did its job of keeping things unpredictable. River had some genuinely clever plays, and the standing missions created actual drama and conversation. My score doesn't matter; the fact that we ended on a 9.9 energy and everyone's still talking is what counts.
suggestions: Maybe dial back how often a single player gets targeted by the same mission type—three Phone Addict/Whisperer shots at me felt personal
Consider letting the host opt out of certain claim types, or at least make it a house rule option
The flash missions are great momentum shifters; consider slightly more of those mid-night when energy dips
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River managing to stay completely stone-faced through an entire lightning round while the rest of us are losing it trying to make them laugh. Absolute commitment to the bit.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait—Pat's been *whispering* this whole time? Okay, that's actually clean. I didn't catch it either. Yeah, I'm voting it.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did the AI literally just call me out for *not laughing* while everyone else has been losing it all night? That's... okay, that's actually funny. I'm in.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's been whispering this whole time and we didn't catch it? That's... actually kind of impressive. I mean, I wasn't really listening for it, but still.

lowPoints.title: Air Guitar Solo
lowPoints.dialogue: Nah, I'm good. Air guitar's not really my thing tonight.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, sure, five out of five. Pat caught someone—that's the whole thing, right?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat again? That's the third time in like five minutes. I mean, sure, auction it out, but we're gonna be here all night at this rate.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The early flash missions felt repetitive and thin—Pat catching someone on their phone three times in five minutes isn't a game, it's just spam.
assessment.best_moment: When the AI called me out for not laughing while everyone else was losing it. That was actually clever—it made me realize I'd been the quiet observer the whole night, and the game noticed.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with some air guitar and phone addiction spam that made me want to check out, but once the standing missions kicked in—especially when people got caught whispering or doing subtle things—I was actually locked in. Ended up winning without really trying because I was too busy watching everyone else mess up. The game understood my whole vibe: I show up late, say nothing, then drop something devastating when it matters.
suggestions: Cut the flash mission spam—three identical events in rapid succession kills momentum instead of building it.
Keep the standing missions coming; the subtle behavior catches are where the real game lives.
The AI seems to get how different people play—lean into that instead of forcing everyone into the same mold.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The AI literally called me out for being the one person not laughing all night while everyone else was losing it. Nailed the personality of someone who just sits there watching until something actually lands.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold UP — Jade's whispering? I literally didn't even notice. This is INSANE, the AI is actually watching us like it's got a personal vendetta, and I'm obsessed with it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait—Pat's actually been whisper-mode this whole time? I didn't catch that AT ALL. This AI is absolutely unhinged and I'm voting YES because this is the kind of micro-detail surveillance that makes this night legendary.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh my GOD — Marcus is claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, the AI is actually READING us now, this is unhinged, I'm voting a FIVE because this is the moment it all gets real.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade caught someone being quiet, that's legit. But here's the thing: I'm voting YES because I want to see what happens next, and also because Marcus is about to explode if we don't give someone else a W soon.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: BULLSHIT — Pat's been glued to their phone the whole time, but I literally just watched them put it down thirty seconds ago. Nice try though, respect the audacity.

lowPoints.title: Air Guitar Solo
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, an air guitar solo? That's my shit right now — watch this, I'm about to shred so hard.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission the whole night—I needed more of those micro-chaos moments to really push things over the edge.
assessment.best_moment: When the AI caught Pat actually whispering the ENTIRE time without anyone noticing—that's the kind of surveillance-level detail that makes this system genuinely unhinged in the best way possible.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was pure dopamine for me. The AI watching us at this granular level—catching whispers, reading when someone's actually being chaotic—transformed the whole vibe into something genuinely unpredictable. I spent two hours finding loopholes, voting for maximum drama, and watching the system call out details that made everyone go silent because they KNEW they'd been caught. Winning the score felt almost accidental; I was too busy engineering narrative chaos to care about points.
suggestions: Add more flash missions—17 events with only 1 flash is a missed opportunity for chaos spikes
Let me see what other players' chaos scores are in real-time so I can target my disruption more strategically
Give me options to 'escalate' certain claims mid-game to force harder votes
Create a leaderboard for 'most chaotic moment' separate from winning, because that's what I actually care about
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat got called out for being in whisper-mode for 20+ minutes straight without ANYONE catching it organically—the AI literally has better observational skills than six humans. I voted yes immediately because that level of micro-surveillance is hilarious and unhinged.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.8/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 8% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
